card_description: Christ (Greek (Christos): "Anointed one") is a divine or mythological figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the biblical tradition, Christ is title for the messiah; the anointed. PKD's theology centers on the living Christ as cosmic principle. Appears in 34 Exegesis passages. Associated with dictionary terms: Christ. Context: "Dick synthesizes the "Magdeburg hemispheres" of his 1974 experience: the external transformation of Fullerton into Rome and the internal transformation of himself into an early Christian Essene."

reference.brief: Title for the messiah; the anointed
reference.significance: PKD's theology centers on the living Christ as cosmic principle
